title: Virtualization, Containers, VDI, and Isolation Boundaries
description: A training lab consolidates legacy applications, disposable practice environments, remote desktops, and development containers, but capacity, licensing, device access, image trust, and isolation assumptions are undocumented. Learners cumulatively produce virtual workload placement and evidence plan through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish type 1 and type 2 hypervisors, guests, virtual hardware, images, snapshots, clones, containers, sandboxes, VDI, and remote sessions.
Match CPU extensions, cores, memory, storage, graphics, network, firmware, operating system, licensing, and workload concurrency to host capacity.
Trace virtual adapters, switches, modes, addresses, routes, shared folders, virtual disks, thin provisioning, snapshots, and underlying failure domains.
